report_name: Silent Ransom Group Claims Phishing Attack on Law Firm Jones Day, Demands $13M

threat_actors.name: Silent Ransom Group
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated threat actor specializing in data theft for extortion, believed to be a splinter group of the Conti syndicate. The group claimed responsibility for the phishing attack against the law firm Jones Day, demanding a $13 million ransom.

threat_actors.name: Conti
threat_actors.description: A notorious ransomware syndicate from which the Silent Ransom Group is considered a descendant or splinter group.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as the initial access vector to compromise the law firm Jones Day, likely through a credential harvesting page or a malicious attachment.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Exfiltration
attack_patterns.description: Likely used by the Silent Ransom Group to exfiltrate valuable client files and internal communications from Jones Day's network.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: A likely method used by the Silent Ransom Group to move stolen data from the victim's network to attacker-controlled cloud storage.

identities.name: Jones Day
identities.description: A prominent global law firm that was the victim of a targeted phishing and data extortion attack by the Silent Ransom Group, resulting in unauthorized access to files for ten clients.

infrastructure.name: Silent Ransom Group Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A public leak site used by the Silent Ransom Group to publish alleged negotiation chats and apply pressure on Jones Day to pay the ransom.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Cloud Storage
infrastructure.description: Cloud storage accounts likely used by the attackers to stage and exfiltrate stolen data from the victim's network.

indicators.name: Potential Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
id: 2b1c9e8d-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects POST or PUT requests to common cloud storage providers, which could be indicative of data exfiltration. This rule is broad and should be tuned for volume and source context in a production environment.
references:
  - https://www.cyberdaily.au/security/10667-ransomware-group-claims-hack-of-legal-giant-jones-day
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-09
tags:
  - attack.t1537
  - attack.exfiltration
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of cloud storage services for file sharing and backup.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|contains:
      - 'mega.nz'
      - 'dropbox.com'
      - 'drive.google.com'
      - 'onedrive.live.com'
    cs-method:
      - 'POST'
      - 'PUT'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects large data uploads to commercial cloud storage (e.g., Mega, Dropbox) from internal workstations, which could indicate data exfiltration as performed by Silent Ransom Group.

relationships.source_name: Potential Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
Potential Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
